channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US warns shippers against paying Strait of Hormuz tolls, ‘donations’
item.description: Latest warning comes as Iranian state media reports Tehran has presented new peace proposal to US.

item.title: Michael Jackson’s biopic and what it leaves out
item.description: A new biopic revisits Michael Jackson - but what’s left out of his story, and who decides his legacy?

item.title: UAE exit from OPEC signals closer alignment with US interests, experts say
item.description: UAE wants to supply more oil than its OPEC quota and that could help push down prices once the Strait of Hormuz opens.

item.title: EU trade deal with South America’s Mercosur bloc takes provisional effect
item.description: The long-awaited deal creates one of the world’s largest free trade areas with 720m potential consumers.

item.title: Pentagon announces deal with seven AI companies for classified systems
item.description: Most recent instance of department integrating with AI comes amid Anthropic standoff, concern over use in Iran war.

item.title: Turkish police fire tear gas, arrest hundreds at May Day rally
item.description: Turkish authorities violently crackdown on May Day demonstrations at Istanbul’s Taksim Square, firing tear gas.

item.title: Italian firefighters respond to Tuscany wildfires
item.description: Wildfires have ravaged over 810 hectares (2,000 acres) of forest in Tuscany.

item.title: Cricket club bans social media comments to protect players’ mental health
item.description: English basement county, Kent, are led by former international Adam Hollioake, who is concerned for players' well being.

item.title: Benfica’s Mourinho says no contact with Real Madrid about manager’s job
item.description: Ex-Real Madrid coach Jose Mourinho linked with a return to Spanish giants to replace Alvaro Arbeloa.

item.title: Howe says Saudi owners’ desire for success at Newcastle remains unchanged
item.description: PIF said on Thursday that it would cut funding of LIV Golf at end of 2026 ​season.

item.title: Woman killed, children injured as Israel attacks Lebanon
item.description: Israel's assault, which has displaced more than one people in Lebanon, escalates despite US brokered 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Trump’s threat: Why cutting US troops in Europe won’t be easy
item.description: Amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war, Trump has threatened to reduce troops in Germany.

item.title: ‘Turbulent and dangerous’: How shipping is the new global battleground
item.description: From Hormuz to Panama, the South China Sea to the Black Sea, geopolitics is rewriting the rules of global shipping.

item.title: Giorgia Meloni’s moral retreat on Gaza
item.description: The Italian PM’s response to Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza reflects not moral conviction, but political calculation.

item.title: Greek Orthodox Church sounds alarm over attacks on Christians in Jerusalem
item.description: Warning follows Jewish man's arrest over brutal attack on nun in Jerusalem this week.

item.title: Gaza aid flotilla vessels taken to Crete after Israeli interception
item.description: Israel’s military reportedly seized 22 vessels sailing among the Global Sumud Flotilla.

item.title: Has the US-Iran ceasefire reset the clock on War Powers Act deadline?
item.description: Defence secretary claims hostilities have ceased, but lawmakers say US forces remain active despite halted air raids.

item.title: Riots erupt over Australian Aboriginal girl’s murder as suspect arrested
item.description: Australian authorities appeal for calm after riots over killing of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Falklands claim: Can Argentina’s Milei use Trump ties to challenge the UK?
item.description: As US-UK relations nosedive, questions are mounting over whether Trump might shift Washington's position on the islands.

item.title: Who profits big from the war on Iran?
item.description: Who profits big from the war on Iran?

item.title: Oil prices rise again with little sign of war on Iran ending
item.description: Brent crude futures hit $126.41 on Thursday - a 5 percent rise for the week.

item.title: Despite growth and pay rises, Greek workers are among the poorest in Europe
item.description: Inflation has robbed them of a third of their income since the post-2009 global financial crisis, statistics show.

item.title: May Day in the age of AI: The new war on workers
item.description: As companies pour billions into AI, the fight for workers’ rights is being rewritten as a fight for the right to work.

item.title: Mohamed Salah ‘deserves big send-off’, says Liverpool boss Slot
item.description: Egypt international Mohamed Salah set to return for Liverpool before the season ends as Anfield exit looms.

item.title: More than 40 Iranian seafarers killed during US-Israeli war: Union leader
item.description: The Iranian Merchant Mariners Syndicate blamed US and Israeli attacks on Iran's ports and commercial fleet for deaths.
